channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Why do rockets curve after launch instead of going straight? The reason is surprising
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 1.6 million-year-old bones reveal how the first humans really got their meat
item.description: Ancient Kenyan fossils reveal early humans employed a dynamic strategy for meat acquisition, blending hunting with opportunistic scavenging. Evidence of tool marks on bones, alongside predator tooth marks, suggests a sophisticated approach to accessing carcasses. This adaptable foraging, including marrow extraction and meat transport, was crucial for survival and brain development, moving beyond the simple hunter-scavenger debate.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: London police form 100 officers unit to protect Jewish community amid rise in antisemitic attacks
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US warns as Asian needle ant reaches over 20 states; experts warn of sharp, painful attacks
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK blast: Woman killed after ex-partner forcefully enters house with explosive device
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 2.3 million metric tons of lithium hidden beneath the Appalachian Mountains could power the US for centuries: Study
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 22-year-old new snooker world champion reveals plans for major purchase worth half his earnings after Crucible win
item.description: Wu Yize became the second-youngest World Snooker Champion after a dramatic 18–17 win over Shaun Murphy at the Crucible, earning £500,000. The 22-year-old reflected on his family’s sacrifices and his early struggles in Sheffield, where he moved at 16. He now plans to invest his prize money in property in Sheffield rather than luxury spending, marking a grounded start to his reign.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: Brampton high school team with several Indian-origin teens beats 23,000 students worldwide to win prestigious space contest
item.description: Brampton's Central Peel Secondary School students, many of Indian origin, clinched the top prize in the 2026 Gerard K. O’Neill Space Settlement Contest. Their innovative 'Saoirse' project proposed a self-sustaining settlement for 10,000 residents at the Mars-Sun L2 Lagrange point. This marks a historic first for a Canadian team in this prestigious global aerospace competition.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: McGregor vs Holloway UFC 329 comeback fight confirmed? EA Sports teases stunning UFC 6 trailer matchup
item.description: Speculation over a Conor McGregor vs Max Holloway rematch for UFC 329 intensified after EA Sports released the UFC 6 trailer featuring a simulated version of their bout, showing Holloway finishing McGregor. The footage, released ahead of International Fight Week, has fuelled talk around McGregor’s potential return after a long injury absence, with Holloway also acknowledging interest in the matchup.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: Top 20 tennis stars including Djokovic and Sabalenka protest French Open prize money as boycott talk grows
item.description: Tensions between top tennis players and the Grand Slams are escalating ahead of the 2026 French Open, with Aryna Sabalenka warning of a potential boycott over prize money and player welfare. Despite a 9.5% increase at Roland Garros, players argue their revenue share continues to decline, citing inadequate consultation, insufficient welfare provisions, and a growing disconnect between tournament earnings and competitor compensation.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk

item.title: North Korea revises constitution, drops references to reunification with South Korea
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak: Spain allows virus-hit cruise ship to sail to Canary Islands
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘I prefer hiring Indians’: Dubai-based Emaar founder Mohamed Alabbar praises work ethic and discipline
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Safety concerns': US to shut Peshawar consulate in Pakistan, to shift operations to Islamabad
item.description: The United States is closing its consulate general in Peshawar due to safety concerns for diplomatic staff and resource allocation. While the physical presence is reduced, the US will maintain engagement with Khyber Pakhtunkhwa through its embassy in Islamabad. This decision follows a wave of unrest in Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump-backed Vivek Ramaswamy wins Republican nomination for Ohio governor
item.description: Indian-origin Republican Vivek Ramaswamy has secured the nomination for Ohio governor, setting up a significant November contest against Democrat Amy Acton. Ramaswamy, endorsed by former President Donald Trump, comfortably won his primary. The race is anticipated to draw national attention as both candidates prepare for the general election.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Cleric involved in talks with Pakistan Taliban shot dead
item.dc:creator: Raj Shekhar Jha

item.title: 'Operation Epic Fury concluded': Trump aide declares 'objectives achieved' in Iran action
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Twitch streamer Amouranth hides for safety as gunfire breaks out near her house, chilling footage surfaces online
item.description: Kaitlyn Siragusa revealed that multiple gunshots were fired outside her home, forcing her to barricade herself inside. She told fans there was “lots of gunfire outside right now” and later confirmed she was “barricaded down and safe for now.” Security footage showed a hooded figure nearby, though involvement remains unclear. The incident has renewed concerns about her safety following past stalking and home invasion threats.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: "Not sure if I will live": Candace Owens drops chilling texts as Charlie Kirk feared for his life before death
item.description: Candace Owens shared alleged messages from Charlie Kirk claiming he feared for his life before his death. In the texts, Kirk spoke about being “wiped out” and said, “Anyway, I am not sure if I will live to see the end of this revolution.” Owens expressed regret for dismissing his concerns and hinted at a possible cover-up, though she provided no verified evidence to support the claim.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Quote of the Day by Usain Bolt: 'I don’t think limits' words of inspiration from the fastest man on Earth
item.description: Usain Bolt’s quote, “I don’t think limits,” reflects a mindset shaped by injuries, setbacks, and discipline throughout his career. Beyond sprinting, it challenges people to resist early self-imposed ceilings, rethink what’s possible, and focus on solutions over judgment. Bolt’s approach emphasizes consistent effort, mental resilience, and refusing to let temporary setbacks define long-term potential, both on and off the track.
item.dc:creator: TOI Sports Desk
